title: Bilirubin (unconjugated)
description: Bilirubin is a yellow bile pigment derived from heme degradation, commonly used as a biochemical marker for liver function and oxidative stress. It exhibits significant antioxidant properties and plays crucial roles in various physiological and pathological processes. Research applications span liver disease, neonatology, enzymology, and emerging therapeutic fields.
synonyms: Bilirubin Ixα, bilirubin free, bilirubina, bilirubine, Unconjugated Bilirubin, BU, cholerythrin, haematoidin, hematoidin, indirect bilirubin, indirect reacting bilirubin, Ixα-bilirubin, NSC26685, UCB, UCBR

title: Calcitonin Salmon
description: Calcitonin Salmon is a potent, bioactive peptide that regulates bone and calcium metabolism by directly inhibiting osteoclastic bone resorption and promoting renal calcium excretion. Its enhanced potency compared to human calcitonin makes it especially valuable in scientific research on bone diseases, calcium homeostasis, and endocrine disorders.
synonyms: Salcatonin, salmon calcitonin, sCT

title: Chymotrypsinogen
description: Chymotrypsinogen is an essential biological precursor for enzyme chymotrypsin, offering a safe, inactive storage form until enzymatic activity is needed. It serves as a biological model, a controlled tool for proteolysis, and an industry-grade protease precursor. Its well-characterized structure and activation mechanism make it invaluable in enzyme research and biotechnological applications.
synonyms: Zymogen of chymotrypsin, pancreatic chymotrypsinogen A

title: Collagen
description: Collagen is the most abundant structural protein in animals, accounting for approximately 30% of total body protein. It plays a fundamental role in maintaining the integrity, strength, and elasticity of tissues.
synonyms: Hydrolyzed collagen, Collagen peptides, Gelatin, Native collagen, Atelocollagen, Type I/II/III collagen, Bovine/Porcine/Fish collagen

title: Cytochrome C
description: Cytochrome C is a highly conserved, heme-containing mitochondrial protein essential in both electron transport and apoptotic signaling. With a molecular weight around 12.4 kDa, it functions by transferring electrons between respiratory complexes III and IV as well as activates caspases upon release into the cytosol. It is widely used in biochemistry, cell biology, and molecular medicine research.
synonyms: CYC, Cytochrome C oxidase substrate, Cyt C, ferrocytochrome C, ferricytochrome C

title: Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH)
description: Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH) is a heterodimeric glycoprotein hormone composed of two subunits and vital for reproductive processes. It plays essential roles in ovarian follicular maturation and testicular spermatogenesis. FSH, whether purified from human urine (urofollitropin) or produced recombinantly (follitropin alpha/beta), is used widely in both clinical treatments such as fertility therapy and in basic research.
synonyms: Follicle-stimulating gonadotropin, follitropin, FSH, hFSH, huFSH, human FSH, recombinant FSH (rFSH, follitropin alpha or beta), urofollitropin (urinary FSH)

title: Gastric Mucin
description: Gastric mucin is a high-molecular-weight glycoprotein primarily responsible for forming the protective mucus barrier in the stomach. It is a complex molecule with extensive glycosylation that grants it viscoelastic, adhesive, and pathogen-trapping properties. In scientific research, gastric mucin is widely used to model the gastrointestinal mucus layer for drug delivery studies, pathogen adhesion analysis, and the development of mucoadhesive biomaterials.
synonyms: Gastric mucus, gastric mucus glycoprotein, gastric surface mucin, glycoprotein mucin, hog gastric mucin, MUC5AC (predominant mucin subtype in gastric mucus), mucin from porcine gastric mucosa, mucin from porcine stomach, PGM, stomach mucin

title: Glucagon
description: Glucagon is a 29-amino acid peptide hormone with a central role in blood glucose regulation. Acting as a counter-regulatory hormone to insulin, it triggers glycogen breakdown and glucose production in the liver via cAMP-mediated pathways. Widely used in clinical practice to reverse hypoglycemia and in research for studying metabolic pathways, glucagon remains a vital tool in diabetes management, hepatic function studies, and pharmacological development.
synonyms: GCG, glucagon hydrochloride, recombinant human glucagon

title: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin (HCG)
description: Human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG) is a vital glycoprotein hormone involved in fertility and pregnancy maintenance, secreted by placental trophoblast cells. Its biochemical action mimics that of LH, stimulating gonadal steroid production. HCG could be used in fertility treatments, ovulation induction, and hormone therapy in hypogonadism.
synonyms: CGH, choriogonin, chorionic gonadotropin, hCG, human chorionic gonadotrophin, gonadotropinum chorionicum

title: Hemin
description: Hemin is a biologically essential heme analog and HO-1 inducer, widely used in research to investigate oxidative stress, iron metabolism, and immune modulation. As a heme source, it reconstitutes or activates hemoproteins and serves in the study of disease mechanisms like anemia, inflammation, and neurodegeneration. Properly stored, Hemin is stable and a powerful tool for both in vitro and in vivo applications.
synonyms: chlorohemin, ferriheme, ferriprotoporphyrin IX chloride, hematin chloride, hemin B, heme chloride, panhematin, protoferriheme, protohemin, iron (III)-protoporphyrin IX chloride

title: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin (HMG)
description: Human menopausal gonadotropin (HMG) is a urinary-derived hormone composed of follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), used to stimulate ovulation and support fertility in assisted reproduction. Its dual action on ovarian follicles and the endocrine axis makes it a key agent in fertility treatment protocols.
synonyms: hMG, menopausal gonadotrophin, menotrophin, menotropins, uHMG (urinary-derived HMG), urinary-derived gonadotrophins

title: Insulin (Porcine)
description: Porcine insulin is the classic pancreas-derived peptide hormone used across metabolism research. It's a 51-aa two-chain disulfide-bonded protein that differs from human insulin by a single B30 alanine. In cells and animals, it activates IR→PI3K/Akt signaling to drive GLUT4 translocation and anabolism, making it indispensable for 3T3-L1 adipogenesis, insulin tolerance testing, and canonical insulin-signaling experiments.
synonyms: Insulin from porcine pancreas; insulin from hog pancreas; insulin (pork), insulin (swine)

title: Lactoferrin
description: Lactoferrin is a naturally occurring iron-binding glycoprotein found in human and bovine milk. Its biological versatility has made it a target of interest in clinical nutrition, infection control, cancer therapy, and bioengineering. It is widely used in research and commercial products, including infant formulas, dietary supplements, pharmaceuticals, and cosmeceuticals.
synonyms: Human lactoferrin (hLF) / bovine lactoferrin (bLF), lactoferrin B, lactoferrine, lactotransferrin (LTF), LF, milk transferrin, serotransferrin-like protein

title: Nisin
description: Nisin is a polycyclic antibacterial lantibiotic peptide derived from Lactococcus lactis. It functions by binding to lipid II and disrupting bacterial membranes, making it a powerful natural preservative in the food industry and a valuable tool in microbiological and pharmaceutical research. Nisin has a strong safety profile, broad activity against Gram-positive bacteria, and is increasingly applied in areas such as drug delivery, wound healing, and anti-biofilm strategies.
synonyms: Lacticin 481, lantibiotic nisin, nisin A, nisin peptide, nisin Z

title: Oxytocin Acetate
description: Oxytocin acetate is a synthetic, pharmaceutically active form of the natural peptide hormone oxytocin, essential for reproductive physiology and social behavior modulation. It functions by activating oxytocin receptors that mediate muscle contractions and emotional regulation. Its applications span clinical medicine, psychological research, and molecular pharmacology.
synonyms: α-hypophamine acetate, oxytocin acetate salt, oxytocin acetate salt hydrate, oxytocin acetate syntocinon, oxytoxin acetate, oxytocic hormone acetate

title: Pregnant Mare Serum Gonadotropin (PMSG)
synonyms: chorionic gonadotropin from pregnant mare serum, pregnant mare serum Gonadotrophin, eCG, equine chorionic gonadotropin, equine gonadotropin, PMSG, serum gonadotropin, Gonadotropinum sericum equinum

title: Protamine Sulfate
description: Protamine sulfate is a multifunctional agent with clinical anticoagulant reversal, nucleic acid-binding, and drug delivery roles. It is widely used in medicine, molecular biology, and biopharmaceutical manufacturing, with a well-established safety and efficacy profile when handled under appropriate conditions.
synonyms: Protamine sulfate salt, heparin antagonist, arginine-rich basic protein, salmine sulfate

title: Rennin (or Chymosin)
description: Rennin, also known as chymosin, is an aspartic endopeptidase primarily responsible for milk coagulation. It has a well-characterized substrate specificity, optimal performance in acidic conditions, and is commercially available from both natural and recombinant sources. Its utility in food science and enzyme research makes it a valuable biotechnological tool.
synonyms: Chymosin, rennase, milk-clotting enzyme

title: Somatostatin
description: Somatostatin is a broad-spectrum inhibitory peptide hormone that plays a vital role in neuroendocrine regulation, cell signaling, and hormonal homeostasis. Its ability to suppress multiple hormonal pathways makes it an indispensable tool in scientific research, especially in endocrinology, oncology, and gastroenterology. Commercially synthesized somatostatin (typically the 14-residue version) is stable under appropriate conditions and widely used in in vitro and in vivo applications to study hormone suppression, signaling cascades, and disease modulation.
synonyms: growth hormone–inhibiting hormone (GHIH), growth hormone release-inhibiting hormone (GHRIH), hypothalamic factor, somatostatin-14 , somatotropin release-inhibiting factor (SRIF), somatotropin release-inhibiting hormone (SRIH), SST, SRIF-14/SRIF-28

title: Aprotinin
description: Aprotinin is a well-characterized, potent serine protease inhibitor derived from bovine lung. With its small size, tight structure, and high stability, it effectively inhibits several key proteases involved in proteolysis, coagulation, and inflammation. It is widely used in biochemistry and biomedical research to protect proteins from degradation and to study protease function and regulation.
synonyms: Basic trypsin inhibitor, bovine pancreatic trypsin inhibitor (BPTI), kallikrein inactivator, kunitz-type trypsin inhibitor, MGC148815, pancreatic trypsin inhibitor, protease inhibitor from bovine lung, PTI, serine protease inhibitor aprotinin, TKI, Trasylol® (brand name), trypsin-kallikrein inhibitor

title: Asparaginase
description: Asparaginase is a hydrolase enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis of the amino acid L-asparagine into L-aspartic acid and ammonia. It is commonly derived from bacterial sources such as Escherichia coli and Erwinia chrysanthemi.
synonyms: L-asparagine amidohydrolase, Colaspase, Elspar, Erwinase, Leunase, Kidrolase

title: Beta-Glucanase
description: Beta-Glucanase refers to a group of enzymes derived from Saccharomyces cerevisiae that catalyze the hydrolysis of β-glucans, which are polysaccharides composed of β-D-glucose units linked primarily by β-1,3 and/or β-1,4 glycosidic bonds. These enzymes play a vital role in breaking down complex glucans into simpler sugars.
synonyms: endo-1,3(4)-β-glucanase, β-1,3-1,4-glucanase, lichenase, β-glucan hydrolase, β-D-glucanase, 1,3-1,4-β-D-glucan 4-glucanohydrolase, mixed-linkage β-glucanase

title: Bromelain
description: Bromelain is a complex of proteolytic enzymes derived from the Ananas comosus (pineapple) plant, primarily extracted from the stem and fruit. As a thiol endopeptidase, bromelain hydrolyzes a wide variety of protein substrates and remains active over a broad pH and temperature range. Due to its multifaceted biochemical properties, bromelain is widely used in biomedical research, biotechnology, food processing, and pharmaceutical applications.
synonyms: Ananase, Pineapple Protease, Bromelin

title: Chymotrypsin
description: Chymotrypsin is a powerful and widely used serine protease with highly selective cleavage activity toward peptide bonds of aromatic amino acids. Its roles in biomedical therapy, protein characterization, and biotech manufacturing make it a vital tool in both research and industry.
synonyms: α-chymotrypsin, chymotrypsin A, pancreatic chymotrypsin

title: Deoxyribonuclease I (DNase I)
description: Deoxyribonuclease I (DNase I) is a DNA-cleaving enzyme widely used in molecular biology, cell biology, and clinical applications. It is primarily sourced from bovine pancreas or produced recombinantly. Functioning optimally at neutral pH and 37°C in the presence of magnesium (Mg²⁺) and calcium (Ca²⁺) ions, it catalyzes hydrolytic cleavage of DNA, producing oligonucleotide fragments. DNase I is essential for removing DNA contamination from RNA samples, preparing single-cell suspensions, studying chromatin accessibility, and treating diseases like cystic fibrosis.
synonyms: Bovine DNase,  deoxyribonucleate endonuclease, deoxyribonucleate 5'-oligonucleotido-hydrolase, DNA endonuclease I, DNase, DNase I, pancreatic deoxyribonuclease, pancreatic DNase, thymonuclease

title: Hyaluronidase
description: Hyaluronidase is a hyaluronic acid-degrading enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis of hyaluronic acid (HA) and related glycosaminoglycans. This enzyme plays a pivotal role in modulating the extracellular matrix (ECM), enhancing tissue permeability, and facilitating molecular diffusion.
synonyms: hyaluronate lyase, hyase, hyase-1, hyalase, mucolase

title: Kallikrein
description: Porcine pancreatic kallikrein is a serine protease purified from porcine pancreas, known for its ability to generate bradykinin from kininogen. The enzyme acts optimally near pH 8.0–8.7, hydrolyzing Arg-X peptide bonds such as BAEE. It serves as a model protease for enzyme kinetics, activation studies, MMP-9 zymogen activation, and cell-based assays - especially those involving neural progenitor proliferation.
synonyms: Bradykininogenase, callicrein, depot-padutin, glumorin, pancreatic kallikrein, kallikrein, kininogenin, kininogenase, kallidinogenase, porcine pancreatic kallikrein, kallikrein from porcine pancreas, kallikrein from hog pancreas, padreatin, padutin

title: Lysozyme
description: Lysozyme is a naturally occurring antibacterial enzyme that plays a vital role in the innate immune system. It catalyzes the hydrolysis of β-1,4-glycosidic bonds in the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls, leading to cell lysis. It is especially effective against Gram-positive bacteria.
synonyms: Muramidase,  N-acetylmuramide glycanhydrolase, egg-white lysozyme, peptidoglycan N-acetylmuramoylhydrolase

title: Pancreatin
description: Pancreatin is a mixture of digestive enzymes extracted primarily from porcine or bovine pancreas. It contains key enzyme components such as amylase, lipase and proteases (e.g., trypsin, chymotrypsin).  Pancreatin is a broad-spectrum enzymatic blend mimicking natural pancreatic secretions. It is widely used for its digestive properties, with proven applications in medicine, food processing, and biological research. Its multi-enzyme composition and predictable enzymatic profile make it essential in treating pancreatic disorders and in simulating digestion systems in research.
synonyms: Pancreatic enzymes, pancreatic extract, pancreatin from porcine pancreas, pancreatin from hog pancreas

title: Papain
description: Papain is a broad-spectrum cysteine protease derived from the latex of the Carica papaya (papaya) fruit. It consists of a single peptide chain with three disulfide bridges. It exhibits strong proteolytic activity across a wide pH range and is highly stable in various biochemical and industrial conditions. Due to its specificity (preference towards arginine, lysine, and phenylalanine), effectiveness, safety profile, and natural origin, papain is widely used in both research and commercial applications.
synonyms: Papaya Proteinase I, Papainase, Papaya Peptidase I, Papayotin, Papainum, Vegetable Pepsin

title: Pepsin
description: Pepsin is a potent acidic protease essential in both biological digestion and industrial protein processing. It is highly valued in research and manufacturing due to its specific activity in acidic conditions and ability to hydrolyze a broad range of proteins efficiently. Proper storage and handling ensure long-term enzyme stability and safety.
synonyms: Gastrase, lactopepsin, pepsin A, pepsin from porcine gastric mucosa, pepsin from hog stomach mucosa, pepsini pulvis, pepsinum

title: Ribonuclease A (RNase A)
description: RNase A is an enzyme that catalyzes the degradation of RNA into smaller components by cleaving phosphodiester bonds. It is widely used in molecular biology, biotechnology, and industrial processes to remove RNA contamination or to study RNA metabolism.
synonyms: RNase A, ribonuclease A, bovine pancreatic ribonuclease, RNase A from bovine pancreas

title: Streptokinase
description: Streptokinase is a plasminogen activator enzyme produced by Streptococcus species. It plays a central role in fibrinolysis by converting plasminogen to plasmin, a serine protease that degrades fibrin clots. Unlike tissue plasminogen activator (tPA), streptokinase acts indirectly, forming a 1:1 complex with plasminogen, which then becomes enzymatically active and initiates a proteolytic cascade resulting in clot breakdown.
synonyms: SK, Streptase®, Varidase®, Streptase-Akzonobel

title: Thrombin
description: Thrombin is a critical serine protease responsible for catalyzing fibrin clot formation and regulating multiple steps in the coagulation cascade. With optimal performance at physiological pH and temperature, it plays indispensable roles in biomedicine, pharmaceutical development, and tissue engineering. Its safety and ease of handling—especially in lyophilized forms—make it a valuable tool in both clinical and research laboratories.
synonyms: Activated Factor II, coagulation Factor IIa, Factor IIa, serine protease thrombin, thrombic enzyme

title: Trypsin-Chymotrypsin
description: Trypsin–Chymotrypsin is a proteolytic enzyme blend containing both trypsin and chymotrypsin, two serine proteases typically derived from porcine or bovine pancreas. The combination is used to enhance proteolysis by broadening substrate specificity, as trypsin cleaves basic amino acids (lysine, arginine) while chymotrypsin targets aromatic residues (phenylalanine, tyrosine, tryptophan).
synonyms: Mixed pancreatic enzymes, pancreatic protease blend, protease complex from pancreas, protease enzyme mix, proteolytic enzyme compound, trypsin-chymotrypsin complex/mixture

title: Trypsin
description: Trypsin is a serine protease enzyme commonly derived from porcine or bovine pancreas. It plays a vital role in protein digestion by cleaving peptide bonds at the carboxyl side of lysine and arginine residues. In biotechnology and biochemistry, trypsin is widely used for protein and cell research due to its highly specific cleavage pattern and compatibility with physiological conditions. Trypsinogen is the inactive precursor of trypsin.
synonyms: Bovine pancreas trypsin, porcine pancreas trypsin, pancreatic protease, pancreatic trypsin, parenzyme, serine protease trypsin, trypsin 1, trypsin serine endopeptidase, trypsinase, trypsinum, trypase

title: Urokinase
description: Urokinase is a human serine protease primarily responsible for the activation of plasminogen into plasmin, leading to fibrin clot breakdown. It plays crucial roles in thrombolysis, tissue remodeling, and cancer biology. With a molecular weight around 54 kDa (single-chain form), it operates optimally at physiological pH and temperature. Urokinase is widely used in clinical therapy for thromboembolic diseases and in research areas including oncology, cell migration, and wound healing.
synonyms: High molecular weight urokinase, plasminogen activator, tissue culture urokinase, U-PA, urochinasi (Italian-derived term), URK, urokinase-type plasminogen activator (uPA), urinary-type plasminogen activator, urokinase plasminogen activator, urokinase-type PA, uroquinase, hUK

title: Coenzyme A Trilithium Salt
description: Coenzyme A trilithium salt is a highly soluble, bioactive lithium salt form of Coenzyme A (CoA), a vital cofactor in numerous metabolic and enzymatic processes. This purified form is especially favored in biochemical research, enzymology, and pharmaceutical applications due to its enhanced stability and compatibility with aqueous systems.
synonyms: Coenzyme A trilithium salt from yeast, CoA-Li, Co A-Li, Co A-SH, CoA trilithium salt, Trilithium Coenzyme A, Trilithium salt of CoA

title: Coenzyme Q10
description: Coenzyme Q10 is a fat-soluble, vitamin-like compound naturally present in the mitochondria of most eukaryotic cells. It plays a critical role in the production of ATP (adenosine triphosphate) via the electron transport chain, acting as an essential electron carrier in oxidative phosphorylation. Coenzyme Q10 also functions as a potent lipophilic antioxidant, protecting cellular membranes and lipoproteins from oxidative damage. CoQ10 is soluble in acetone, chloroform and ether, insoluble in water.
synonyms: Ubiquinone-10, Ubiquinone-50, CoQ10, Ubidecarenone, Q10

title: Coenzyme A (Free Acid)
description: Coenzyme A (CoA) is a vital metabolic cofactor found in all living cells, functioning primarily as an acyl group carrier. It plays a central role in the metabolism of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins by facilitating the synthesis and oxidation of fatty acids and the oxidation of pyruvate in the citric acid cycle. Coenzyme A is often referred to as Acetyl CoA when it is transferring an acetyl group.
synonyms: CoA, Co-A, CoASH, Co-A-SH, SH-CoA, HS-CoA, Coenzyme A hydrate, Coenzymum A, Coenzyme A (free acid) from yeast, Acetyl CoA, Coenzyme for transacetylation

title: Beta-Glucans
description: Beta-Glucans are a class of natural polysaccharides composed primarily of D-glucose units linked by β-glycosidic bonds. The primary linkages are β-(1→3), β-(1→4), or β-(1→6), depending on the biological source (yeast, fungi, oats, barley, or bacteria).
synonyms: β-D-glucan, β-1,3/1,6-glucan, oats β-glucan, barley β-glucan, yeast β-glucan

title: Chitosan Hydrochloride
description: Chitosan hydrochloride is the water-soluble, protonated salt of chitosan, a biodegradable, biocompatible cationic linear polysaccharide. It carries abundant –NH₃⁺ groups in aqueous media, enabling strong electrostatic interactions with anionic biomolecules and cell surfaces. These properties underpin its wide use as a mucoadhesive excipient, antimicrobial/hemostatic agent, and polycation for gene/drug delivery and colloid/flocculation applications.
synonyms: Chitosan HCl, deacetylated chitin hydrochloride, poliglusam hydrochloride, water-soluble chitosan

title: Chitosan Oligosaccharide
description: Chitosan oligosaccharide (COS) is a low-molecular-weight, water-soluble derivative of chitosan with excellent biocompatibility, biodegradability, and bioactivity. Its small size and cationic nature enable superior solubility, improved bioavailability, and versatile functionality in biomedical, food, agricultural, and cosmetic research.
synonyms: Chitooligosaccharide, chitooligomer, chitosan oligos, COS, deacetylated chitin oligosaccharide, oligochitosan

title: Chitosan
description: Chitosan is a natural, biodegradable polysaccharide derived by the deacetylation of chitin, primarily sourced from crustacean shells (e.g., shrimp, crab). It consists of randomly distributed β-(1→4)-linked D-glucosamine and N-acetyl-D-glucosamine units.
synonyms: Chitosan is a natural, biodegradable polysaccharide derived by the deacetylation of chitin, primarily sourced from crustacean shells (e.g., shrimp, crab). It consists of randomly distributed β-(1→4)-linked D-glucosamine and N-acetyl-D-glucosamine units.

title: Chondroitin Sulfate
description: Chondroitin Sulfate is a sulfated glycosaminoglycan (GAG) essential for the structural integrity of cartilage and connective tissue. Derived mainly from animal cartilage, it plays a central role in maintaining joint function, preventing cartilage breakdown, and modulating inflammation. In scientific research and the pharmaceutical industry, it is extensively used for developing treatments for osteoarthritis, tissue regeneration, and targeted drug delivery.
synonyms: chondroitin polysulfate, chondroitin sulfuric acid, chondroitin sulphate, chonsurid, CSA (chondroitin sulfate A), CSC (chondroitin sulfate C), CSD (chondroitin sulfate D), β-(1→3)-N-cetylgalactosamine-(1→4)-glucuronic acid polysulfate

title: Dermatan Sulfate
description: Dermatan sulfate is a sulfated glycosaminoglycan structurally related to chondroitin sulfate but rich in iduronic acid. It plays crucial roles in hemostasis, wound healing, and extracellular matrix regulation, primarily through interactions with heparin cofactor II, TGF-β, and other signaling molecules.
synonyms: Chondroitin sulfate B (CS-B), dermatan polysulfate, DS, mucopolysaccharide B

title: Dextran Sulfate Sodium (DSS)
description: Dextran Sulfate Sodium (DSS) is a sulfated polysaccharide derivative of dextran, composed primarily of glucose units sulfated at multiple positions. It is water-soluble, biocompatible and carries a high negative charge due to its sulfate groups.
synonyms: DSS, sodium dextran sulfate, dextran sulfate sodium (DSS) salt, polysulfated dextran sodium

title: Dextran
description: Dextran is a high-molecular-weight polysaccharide composed predominantly of α-(1→6)-linked D-glucose units, with varying degrees of α-(1→3) branching. It is naturally synthesized by certain strains of Leuconostoc, Lactobacillus, and Streptococcus through the action of the enzyme dextransucrase, which uses sucrose as a substrate. Dextran is soluble in water, slightly soluble in ethanol.
synonyms: glucose polymer, poly(glucopyranose), α-D-glucan, poly-D-glucose, Polyglucan, Dextran 40/70/500 (by MW), Polydextrose (sometimes loosely)

title: Dextrose
description: Dextrose is a simple sugar and a primary monosaccharide that serves as a crucial source of energy in biological systems. It is naturally occurring and metabolically vital in most organisms.
synonyms: D-glucose, corn sugar, glucose monohydrate, grape sugar

title: Glucosamine Hydrochloride
description: Glucosamine hydrochloride is a naturally occurring amino sugar and a key precursor in the biosynthesis of glycosaminoglycans, which are essential components of cartilage, tendons, and synovial fluid.
synonyms: D-glucosamine HCl, glucosamine monohydrochloride, 2-amino-2-deoxy-D-glucose hydrochloride, chitosamine hydrochloride

title: Glucosamine Sulfate Potassium Chloride
description: Glucosamine Sulfate Potassium Chloride is the potassium salt form of glucosamine sulfate, a naturally occurring amino sugar found in cartilage. It is widely recognized for its chondroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and joint health-promoting properties.
synonyms: D-glucosamine sulfate potassium salt, glucosamine sulfate 2KCl, potassium glucosamine sulfate, GS-KCl

title: Glucosamine Sulfate Sodium Chloride
description: Glucosamine Sulfate Sodium Chloride (GS-NaCl) is a stable, crystalline salt of glucosamine sulfate, combined with sodium chloride to enhance solubility and maintain the sulfate form's stability. It is widely used in pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, and scientific research, particularly for its benefits in joint health and inflammation regulation.
synonyms: D-glucosamine sulfate sodium chloride salt, glucosamine sulfate NaCl, sodium salt of glucosamine sulfate, GS-NaCl

title: Heparan Sulfate
description: Heparan sulfate is a biologically active glycosaminoglycan present in all animal tissues, typically found on cell surfaces and within the extracellular matrix. With diverse and intricate sulfation patterns, HS plays essential roles in cell signaling, development, tissue regeneration, and host–pathogen interactions.
synonyms: N-Acetylheparan sulfate, glycosaminoglycan heparan sulfate, heparan polysulfate, heparan sulphate, heparin sulfate, hepartin sulfate, HS

title: Heparin Calcium
description: Heparin calcium is a calcium salt form of unfractionated heparin (UFH), a complex glycosaminoglycan widely used for its anticoagulant and pleiotropic bioactivities. Compared to the sodium salt, it offers more stable subcutaneous absorption, making it suitable for prolonged anticoagulant therapy.
synonyms: Calcium heparin, calcium heparinate, heparin Ca, heparin calcium salt, heparinum calcium, nadroparin

title: Heparin Sodium
description: Heparin sodium is a highly sulfated glycosaminoglycan derived from animal tissue, with strong anticoagulant properties primarily through antithrombin III activation. Besides its clinical use in thrombosis prevention, it is widely applied in biomedical research, particularly in coagulation biology, drug delivery, angiogenesis inhibition, and tissue engineering.
synonyms: Glycosaminoglycan heparin, heparin sodium salt, heparin sodium salt from porcine intestinal mucosa, heparin sulfate, sodium heparin

title: Heparinoid
description: Heparinoid is a class of heparin-like glycosaminoglycans including heparan sulfate, dermatan sulfate and chondroitin sulfate. Heparinoid is generally less heavily sulfated. Like heparin, heparinoids are widely used in scientific and medical research to explore extracellular matrix dynamics, growth factor signaling, and vascular biology.
synonyms: Glycosaminoglycan heparinoid, heparinoid substance, mucopolysaccharide heparinoid, non-anticoagulant heparin-like compound

title: Hyaluronic Acid Sodium Salt
description: Hyaluronic acid is a naturally occurring, high-molecular-weight glycosaminoglycan composed of repeating disaccharide units of D-glucuronic acid and N-acetyl-D-glucosamine. It plays a critical role in maintaining extracellular matrix integrity, hydration, and tissue repair. It can be hydrolyzed by hyaluronidase. Purified hyaluronic acid is neutralized with sodium hydroxide (NaOH) to form sodium hyaluronate.
synonyms: hyaluronic acid, HA, hyaluronan, sodium hyaluronate

title: Low Molecular Weight Heparins (LMWH)
casNo: enoxaparin sodium 9041-08-1, dalteparin sodium 9041-93-4, tinzaparin sodium 9041-08-1, nadroparin calcium 37270-89-6
description: Low Molecular Weight Heparins (LMWHs) are a class of anticoagulant substance derived from unfractionated heparin (UFH). They are widely used in clinical medicine and biomedical research for their antithrombotic properties, ease of administration (subcutaneous), predictable dose-response, and favorable safety profile.
synonyms: Antithrombotic heparins, fragmented heparin, heparin fragments, mini-heparin

title: Cholecalciferol (vitamin D₃)
description: Cholecalciferol (vitamin D₃) is a lipophilic prohormone (C₂₇H₄₄O, 384.64 g/mol) typically produced by UV conversion of lanolin-derived 7-dehydrocholesterol. After metabolic activation to calcitriol, it binds VDR to regulate genes controlling calcium absorption, bone health, and diverse extra-skeletal pathways. In research, it’s used from basic VDR signaling studies to nutrition, immunology, oncology, and analytical assay development. Because it is highly light/oxygen-sensitive, store it cold in amber, airtight containers, under inert gas when possible, and prepare protected aliquots for consistent activity.
synonyms: Activated 7-dehydrocholesterol, calciol, colecalciferol, (3β,5Z,7E)-9,10-secocholesta-5,7,10(19)-trien-3-ol, Vit D3

title: Estriol
description: Estriol is a naturally occurring estrogen with lower potency and a safer profile compared to estradiol. It plays a significant role during pregnancy,  Estriol is widely studied in the context of hormone therapy, menopause, autoimmune regulation, and urogenital health. Due to its unique receptor dynamics, it offers selective tissue effects, making it an attractive candidate for targeted estrogen therapy in both clinical and experimental settings.
synonyms: oestriol, E3, estra-1,3,5(10)-triene-3,16α,17β-triol, (16α,17β)-estra-1,3,5(10)-triene-3,16,17-triol, 16α-hydroxyestradiol, oestriol, theelol, trihydroxyestrin; trihydroxyoestrin

title: Rosemary Extract
description: Rosemary extract is a standardized botanical mixture from R. officinalis rich in phenolic diterpenes (notably carnosic acid and carnosol) and polyphenols (e.g., rosmarinic acid). As a natural antioxidant (E392) it protects lipid systems from oxidation and, in biomedical research, modulates redox and inflammatory pathways—principally activating Nrf2 and suppressing NF-κB. Applications span food preservation models, redox biology, neuroprotection, antimicrobial studies, and microbiome/metabolic work. It is also a subject of ongoing scientific research for its potential neuroprotective, antitumor, and anti-inflammatory effects. Being a UVCB, it has no single molecular formula; researchers should report composition/standardization and follow SDS-specific storage (cool, dry, light-protected; tighter controls for purified actives).
synonyms: Extracts of rosemary (EU food additive E392), extract of rosemary leaves, rosemary antioxidant, Rosmarinus officinalis extract, Rosmarinus officinalis leaf extract, R. officinalis leaf/flower/leaf-flower-stem extract, rosemary oil extract, Rosmarinus officinalis L. extract

title: Sodium Cholate
description: Sodium cholate is a versatile, biologically relevant bile salt used widely in biochemical, pharmaceutical, and industrial applications. Its amphipathic structure enables it to emulsify lipids, making it essential for membrane protein research, drug delivery system development, and bioprocessing.
synonyms: bile salt sodium cholate, cholate sodium salt, cholic acid sodium salt, sodium 3α,7α,12α-trihydroxy-5β-cholan-24-oate, sodium cholate hydrate, sodium cholate monohydrate
